SEARCH
Package ru.nsu.krasnyanskii.snake.model

Class Snake

java.lang.Object
ru.nsu.krasnyanskii.snake.model.Snake
public class Snake extends Object
Manages the snake body as an ordered sequence of grid cells.

The body is stored in an ArrayDeque: peekFirst() is the head, peekLast() is the tail. A parallel HashSet mirrors the deque so that self-collision lookups run in O(1).

Movement algorithm:

Prepend the new head position (addFirst). If pendingGrowth > 0, decrement it and skip tail removal — the snake grows. Otherwise, remove the tail segment (removeLast) — the snake slides.

This class is completely free of JavaFX and has no knowledge of board boundaries. Boundary logic is handled by BoardBounds before move() is called.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Snake(Point startPosition)
    Creates a snake with a single segment at startPosition.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    contains(Point point)
    Checks whether the given point overlaps any segment of this snake.
    List<Point>
    getBody()
    Returns an unmodifiable snapshot of the body in head-first order.
    Point
    getHead()
    Returns the head cell (front of the body).
    int
    getLength()
    Returns the current number of segments.
    Point
    getTail()
    Returns the tail cell (back of the body).
    Point
    move(Direction direction)
    Moves the snake one step in direction.
    void
    scheduleGrowth(int delta)
    Schedules a change in snake length to be applied over subsequent move(ru.nsu.krasnyanskii.snake.model.entity.Direction) calls.
    boolean
    selfCollision()
    Detects self-collision by checking whether the current head appears more than once in the body (which happens when the snake loops back into itself after a move).
    void
    teleportHead(Point newHead)
    Replaces the head cell without changing the body length.

  • Constructor Details

    • Snake

      public Snake(Point startPosition)
      Creates a snake with a single segment at startPosition.
      Parameters:
      startPosition - the initial head cell

  • Method Details

    • move

      public Point move(Direction direction)
      Moves the snake one step in direction.

      The caller is responsible for supplying an already-validated head position via Point.move(Direction) and BoardBounds.apply(Point) before calling this method.

      Parameters:
      direction - the direction to move
      Returns:
      the tail Point that was removed, or null if the snake grew

    • scheduleGrowth

      public void scheduleGrowth(int delta)
      Schedules a change in snake length to be applied over subsequent move(ru.nsu.krasnyanskii.snake.model.entity.Direction) calls.

      Positive delta queues growth segments. Negative delta immediately removes tail segments, clamped so the snake always retains at least one segment.

      Parameters:
      delta - segments to add (positive) or remove (negative)

    • contains

      public boolean contains(Point point)
      Checks whether the given point overlaps any segment of this snake.
      Parameters:
      point - any grid cell
      Returns:
      true if point overlaps any segment of this snake

    • selfCollision

      public boolean selfCollision()
      Detects self-collision by checking whether the current head appears more than once in the body (which happens when the snake loops back into itself after a move).
      Returns:
      true if the head overlaps any body segment behind it

    • getHead

      public Point getHead()
      Returns the head cell (front of the body).
      Returns:
      the head cell (front of the body)

    • getTail

      public Point getTail()
      Returns the tail cell (back of the body).
      Returns:
      the tail cell (back of the body)

    • getLength

      public int getLength()
      Returns the current number of segments.
      Returns:
      the current number of segments

    • getBody

      public List<Point> getBody()
      Returns an unmodifiable snapshot of the body in head-first order. Consumed by the view layer for rendering only.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of body cells, head at index 0

    • teleportHead

      public void teleportHead(Point newHead)
      Replaces the head cell without changing the body length. Used for wrap-around.
